name: R.R. Street & Co.

overview.summary: R.R. Street & Co. is the dominant manufacturer and marketer of dry-cleaning process additives, stain removal agents, and filtration products in North America. Founded in 1876, Street's is known for flagship products like StreeTANÂź (tannin spotter), StreePROÂź (protein spotter), and PICRINÂź (volatile dry-side spotter), and distributes ExxonMobil's DF-2000âą hydrocarbon solvent. They serve professional dry cleaners, commercial laundries, and hospitality businesses through a nationwide distributor network.
overview.historyAndReputation: Robert R. Street founded the company in Chicago in 1876, making it one of the oldest continuously operating chemical suppliers in the dry cleaning industry â over 140 years of operation. The company built its position through consistent product quality, comprehensive spotting agent lines, and strong distributor relationships. Their 2014 acquisition of Adco/Laidlaw brands consolidated their market leadership.
overview.marketPosition: Market leader in North American dry cleaning chemicals. Premium-to-mid-market pricing. National distribution through authorized supply houses. The majority of professional dry cleaners in the US have used or currently use at least one Street's product. Their training program (Street's Academy) and multilingual support reinforce loyalty across diverse operator demographics.

specialties: Dry cleaning detergents (perc, hydrocarbon, GreenEarth systems)

productLines.categoryDescription: The primary cleaning medium in a dry cleaning machine. The solvent dissolves and carries away oil-based soils from garments. Choice of solvent determines the entire cleaning system â detergents, spotters, and equipment must all be compatible.

products.name: DF-2000âą Fluid
products.description: High-flash-point hydrocarbon solvent manufactured by ExxonMobil and distributed by R.R. Street. Introduced in 1994, DF-2000 has the lowest aromatic content of any HFHC dry cleaning solvent, which makes it gentler on dyes, trims, and beading than older hydrocarbon formulations. Flash point exceeds 130Â°F. Remains stable through multiple distillation and reclamation cycles without degradation. No moisture buildup means no shrinkage or water-soluble dye bleeding.
notFor: not a spotting agent â used as machine solvent only
products.proTips: DF-2000 cleans more gently than perc, which means heavily soiled items may need pre-spotting or a second run. Pair with MERASOLÂź detergent for optimal soil removal. Monitor solvent clarity â distill when KB value drops.

productLines.categoryDescription: Wet-side spotting agents that break down protein-based stains â blood, milk, egg, perspiration, baby formula, urine, food proteins. Applied on the spotting board before or after machine cleaning.

products.name: StreePROÂź
products.description: Quick-acting wet-side stain remover with strong detergent qualities for protein-based stains. Removes food, milk, blood, perspiration, baby formula, and urine. Can be used alternately with StreeTANÂź to tackle ballpoint ink, indelible ink, marking pen, and typewriter ribbon stains through an alternating acid-neutral technique.
products.proTips: For stubborn combination stains, alternate between StreePROÂź and StreeTANÂź â the pH shift between neutral protein spotter and acidic tannin spotter can break down complex stains that neither agent handles alone.
products.alternativeTo: A.L. Wilson QwikGoÂź, Pariser protein spotters

productLines.categoryDescription: Acidified wet-side spotting agents for beverage and plant-based stains â coffee, tea, wine, beer, grass, soft drinks, mustard, fruit juices. Tannins are polyphenolic compounds that bond to fibers through hydrogen bonding and require acidic chemistry to break.

products.name: StreeTANÂź
products.description: Acidified tannin spotter that is heat-stable â its activity does not increase at high temperatures, which means it won't damage heat-sensitive fabrics or dyes during steam flushing. Completely water-soluble for easy flushing. Removes coffee, beer, grass, liquor, mustard, soft drinks, tea, and wine. No need to rinse with cold water before steam flushing, which saves time on the board.
fibers: safe for most fabrics including delicates due to heat stability
notFor: not for protein stains â use StreePROÂź instead
products.proTips: The heat stability is the key differentiator. Most tannin spotters become more aggressive with heat, risking dye damage on delicates. StreeTANÂź lets you steam flush immediately without a cold-water rinse step â saves 30-60 seconds per garment on the board.
products.alternativeTo: A.L. Wilson BonGoÂź, Pariser Para-Tann, General Formula No. 209Âź

products.name: General Formula No. 209Âź
products.description: Professional-grade tannin spotter formulated for experienced spotters. More aggressive than StreeTANÂź â effective on tannin stains, caramelized sugar stains, ink, grass, lipstick, and fruit stains. Designed for both wetcleaning and drycleaning applications. Requires more skill to use safely than StreeTANÂź.
fibers: standard fabrics â test on delicates
notFor: inexperienced spotters should use StreeTANÂź instead
products.proTips: 209 is the escalation agent when StreeTANÂź doesn't fully resolve a tannin stain. Use with caution on silk and acetate â the stronger acid formulation requires faster flushing.

productLines.categoryDescription: Volatile solvent-based spotters for oil, grease, paint, tar, adhesive, and other non-polar stains. Applied on the dry side of the spotting board. Must evaporate completely to avoid rings.

products.name: PICRINÂź
products.description: Volatile dry-side spotter with controlled evaporation rate for heavy oily stains. Dissolves adhesives, asphalt, crayon, grease, inks, lipstick, mascara, nail polish, oil, paint, shoe polish, tar, and varnish. Evaporates completely â leaves no residue and facilitates feathering to prevent rings.
fibers: most fibers â volatile formula minimizes fiber contact time
notFor: acetate (test first â some volatile solvents attack acetate)
products.proTips: PICRINÂź can be combined with STREETEXÂź to create the 2-1 FORMULAÂź â a wet/dry combination spotter for mystery stains. The controlled evaporation rate is key: too-fast evaporation means the solvent leaves before dissolving the stain; too-slow means rings. PICRINÂź is calibrated for optimal dwell.

products.name: STREETEXÂź
products.description: Versatile agent that works as a water-based pre-spotter on its own, or combines with PICRINÂź to create the 2-1 FORMULAÂź wet/dry combination spotter. Removes mystery stains, embedded soil, and latex paint. The combination approach tackles stains with both polar and non-polar components simultaneously.
products.proTips: When you don't know what the stain is, start with the 2-1 FORMULAÂź (PICRINÂź + STREETEXÂź). It attacks both wet-side and dry-side components simultaneously. This is the go-to mystery stain approach for many Street's-trained spotters.

productLines.categoryDescription: Applied before machine cleaning to boost stain removal without board spotting, or used as leveling agents to prevent water marks and rings on moisture-sensitive fabrics. Save labor by reducing post-spotting.

products.name: MultiSPOTÂź
products.description: No-flush pre-treatment, leveling agent, and cleaning booster for perchloroethylene systems. Spray on garments before loading â no board flushing required. Targets unidentified and combination stains: oily foods, cosmetics, inks, embedded soil. Rinses easily in the wheel during the cleaning cycle.
notFor: not for hydrocarbon or GreenEarth systems â use GreenSPOTÂź instead
products.proTips: MultiSPOTÂź is a labor-saver. Instead of board-spotting every collar and cuff, spray MultiSPOTÂź on problem areas and load the machine. It handles 70-80% of routine soil that would otherwise require board time.

products.name: GreenSPOTÂź
products.description: Pre-treatment and leveling agent formulated specifically for GreenEarthÂź solvent systems. Same no-flush spray-and-clean concept as MultiSPOTÂź but chemistry is optimized for silicone-based D5 solvent compatibility.
fibers: all fabrics processed in GreenEarth systems
notFor: perc systems â use MultiSPOTÂź instead
products.proTips: GreenEarth's D5 solvent is less aggressive than perc, so pre-spotting becomes more important. GreenSPOTÂź bridges the gap between D5's gentleness and the cleaning power needed for real-world soil.

products.name: SOFSPOTÂź
products.description: Gentle stain remover for water-soluble stains on delicate fabrics. Explicitly safe for silk, rayon, wool, linen, and cotton. Targets food, beverages, and perspiration stains on garments that cannot tolerate aggressive spotting chemistry.
notFor: oil-based stains â use PICRINÂź or AquaSOLVÂź for those
products.proTips: SOFSPOTÂź is the safe choice when you're not sure if a fabric can handle StreePROÂź or StreeTANÂź. It's milder but slower â plan for longer dwell times on stubborn stains.

productLines.categoryDescription: Detergents and spotting agents designed specifically for professional wet cleaning â the process of washing dry-clean-only garments in water using specialized equipment, detergents, and techniques to prevent shrinkage, stretching, and dye loss.

products.name: H2PROÂź Detergent
products.description: Complete wet cleaning detergent system formulated for multiple applications: hand washing, top-loading machines, front-loading machines, professional laundry, and dedicated wetcleaning machines. Uses latest advancements in surfactant chemistry to clean effectively while protecting fibers and dyes.
fibers: most dry-clean-only fabrics when wet cleaned properly
notFor: not a dry cleaning detergent â for aqueous systems only
products.proTips: Wet cleaning is growing fast as shops move away from perc. H2PROÂź is Street's answer to Kreussler's Lanadol system. Match it with proper equipment (Miele, Electrolux, etc.) and controlled drying for best results.
products.alternativeTo: Kreussler Lanadol AKTIV, Seitz Viva Care

products.name: AquaSOLVÂź
products.description: Pre-spotting agent formulated for wet cleaning processes. Removes solvent-soluble stains (oils, greases) before garments enter the aqueous wet cleaning cycle. Critical because water alone won't dissolve oil-based stains â AquaSOLVÂź handles the dry-side component before the wet-side wash.
usedFor: pre-spotting oily stains before wet cleaning
removing solvent-soluble soils in aqueous systems
products.proTips: Apply AquaSOLVÂź to oil-based stains before loading into the wet cleaning machine. Water rinses it away along with the dissolved oils. This two-step approach (AquaSOLVÂź pre-spot + H2PROÂź wash) handles most stain types in a wet cleaning workflow.
products.alternativeTo: Traditional POG applied before wet cleaning

products.name: WetDryÂź
products.description: Combines wet-side and dry-side stain removal agents in one pre-mixed, low-odor product. Eliminates the need to switch between separate wet and dry spotters for combination stains.
usedFor: mystery stains in wet cleaning environments
products.proTips: WetDryÂź is the wet cleaning equivalent of the 2-1 FORMULAÂź concept. One product for both components of a mystery stain.
products.alternativeTo: Separate wet and dry spotters used in sequence

productLines.categoryDescription: Charge soaps and injection detergents added to the dry cleaning machine's solvent to improve soil removal, prevent redeposition, reduce static, and control moisture. Different formulations for different solvent systems.

products.name: MERASOLÂź
products.description: Concentrated detergent formula for hydrocarbon solvent systems (DF-2000âą). Solubilizes moisture in solvent so water-soluble soils can be dissolved and flushed away during the cleaning cycle. Prevents moisture buildup that causes shrinkage.
notFor: not for perc or GreenEarth systems
products.proTips: Proper MERASOLÂź dosing is critical. Too little and water-soluble soils stay on garments. Too much and you get moisture problems. Follow the dosing chart for your machine size and load weight.

products.name: GreenSOLÂź
products.description: Detergent formulated specifically for GreenEarthÂź D5 silicone solvent systems. Provides stain and soil removal beyond what the solvent alone achieves. Excellent soil suspension prevents redeposition. Reduces lint and static.
notFor: not for perc or hydrocarbon systems
products.proTips: D5 silicone is a gentle solvent â it relies more heavily on the detergent for actual soil removal than perc does. Don't under-dose GreenSOLÂź or you'll see dingy results.

agentMapping.NSD: No dedicated NSD-branded product. MERASOLÂź and GreenSOLÂź serve the detergent role. SOFSPOTÂź functions as a neutral wet-side spotter for delicates.
agentMapping.POG: PICRINÂź (volatile dry spotter), STREETEXÂź (combination), 2-1 FORMULAÂź (PICRINÂź + STREETEXÂź)
agentMapping.rustRemover: No dedicated rust remover identified in current product line
agentMapping.enzymatic: No dedicated enzyme product identified in current product line
agentMapping.solvent: DF-2000âą Fluid (hydrocarbon), plus formulations for perc and GreenEarth systems

fiberExpertise.silk: SOFSPOTÂź is explicitly formulated safe for silk. StreeTANÂź's heat stability makes it safer for silk than most tannin spotters â no risk of heat activation damaging silk proteins during steam flushing.
fiberExpertise.wool: SOFSPOTÂź safe for wool. H2PROÂź wet cleaning system can handle wool with proper machine settings. StreeTANÂź heat stability is an advantage on wool.
fiberExpertise.leather: Leather care category exists in product portfolio but specific product names are not prominently marketed. Adco/Laidlaw acquisition may include leather-specific formulations.
fiberExpertise.synthetics: Full product line compatible with synthetics. No special considerations â synthetics are the easiest fibers for most Street's products.
fiberExpertise.delicates: SOFSPOTÂź is the dedicated delicate fabric spotter. GreenSPOTÂź and MultiSPOTÂź provide leveling to prevent water marks on moisture-sensitive fabrics. Street's training program emphasizes delicate handling techniques.

protocols.name: 2-1 FORMULAÂź (Mystery Stain Protocol)
protocols.description: Combine PICRINÂź and STREETEXÂź to create a wet/dry combination spotter that attacks both polar and non-polar stain components simultaneously. Apply to unknown stains, work with spatula or brush, steam flush. The combination handles most mystery stains without needing to identify the stain type first.
protocols.usedFor: Mystery stains, combination stains, embedded soil, unknown stain identification

protocols.name: StreePROÂź/StreeTANÂź Alternating Protocol
protocols.description: For stubborn combination stains (especially ballpoint ink and marker), alternate between StreePROÂź (protein/neutral) and StreeTANÂź (acidic/tannin). The pH shift between applications breaks down complex stain chemistry that neither agent handles alone. Apply StreePROÂź, flush, apply StreeTANÂź, flush, repeat.
protocols.usedFor: Ballpoint ink, indelible ink, marking pen, typewriter ribbon, stubborn combination stains

protocols.name: No-Flush Pre-Treatment (Labor-Saving)
protocols.description: Spray MultiSPOTÂź (perc) or GreenSPOTÂź (GreenEarth) on collars, cuffs, and visibly soiled areas before loading into the machine. No board flushing required â agents dissolve in the solvent during the cleaning cycle. Handles routine soil that would otherwise require individual board spotting.
protocols.usedFor: Collar soil, cuff grime, routine embedded soil, labor reduction

distributionNetwork.howToBuy: Contact R.R. Street at (630) 416-4244 or info@4streets.com for the nearest authorized distributor. Products are not sold direct â all sales go through the distribution network. Major distributors include FabriClean Supply, Elevation Supplies, and Garland Supply.

strengthsKnownFor: Most comprehensive spotting agent portfolio in North America
Heat-stable StreeTANÂź tannin spotter (unique differentiator â no cold-water pre-rinse needed)
Strong training program (Street's Academy, multilingual, on-site)
Acquired Adco/Laidlaw to consolidate market position
limitations: No dedicated rust remover or enzyme product in current public product line
Privately held â limited public information about company financials and strategy
Products available only through distributors â no direct purchase option
industryReputation.whoUsesThem: The broadest customer base of any dry cleaning chemical supplier in North America. Used by single-location family dry cleaners, multi-unit operations, commercial laundries, and hospitality businesses. Strong adoption among Korean-American operators (Korean-language training available). GreenEarth-compatible line gives access to eco-focused shops.

education.trainingResources: Street's Academy provides free online training with expert-led instruction. 'On the Spotâą' video series covers stain removal techniques with step-by-step tutorials. Available anytime online.
education.technicalSupport: On-site seminars with hands-on training at customer facilities. SpotStation equipment shipped to customer sites for training sessions. Instruction in English, Spanish, and Korean. Customized training for franchises, new staff onboarding, and process optimization.
education.publications: Online guides and video library through Street's Academy. Product-specific usage guides included with products.

gonrIntegration.relevance: R.R. Street is the most widely available professional chemical line in North America. When GONR protocols call for professional-grade spotting agents, Street's products are the most accessible option for the majority of US-based professional cleaners. Their heat-stable StreeTANÂź and versatile 2-1 FORMULAÂź approach align directly with GONR's protocol methodology.
gonrIntegration.agentCrossReference: When a GONR protocol calls for: protein spotter â StreePROÂź; tannin spotter â StreeTANÂź (standard) or General Formula No. 209Âź (advanced); POG/dry-side spotter â PICRINÂź; mystery stain â 2-1 FORMULAÂź (PICRINÂź + STREETEXÂź); delicate fabric spotter â SOFSPOTÂź; pre-treatment â MultiSPOTÂź (perc) or GreenSPOTÂź (GreenEarth); wet cleaning detergent â H2PROÂź.
gonrIntegration.whenToRecommend: Default recommendation for professional cleaners in the US who need a comprehensive, readily available spotting system. Especially strong for: shops running hydrocarbon (DF-2000âą) systems, GreenEarth affiliates needing compatible chemistry, operations that need multilingual training support, and any shop looking for the widest product selection from a single supplier.
